rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE annexes; avoid package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component ports or implementation details.
Do not introduce new data types, identifiers, or property references that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
When writing assumptions or guarantees, use only named constants or visible features from the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the scope of the annex.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not assume numeric or Boolean encodings for event ports unless explicitly defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex structure (covered in KSYN-1)
Validator diagnostics and repair patterns (covered in KDEF)
Requirement semantics and traceability (covered in KEXP)
